spec_match.rationale: The repaired page surfaces border-width, outline-width, column-rule-width, corresponding style properties, computedStyleMap(), and getComputedStyle() in a live contract probe.

interactive.rationale: Duration, box count, style-flip, pause, authored-width, and style controls all drive live animation or CSSOM readout changes.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The animation path now carries a registered --edge-width custom-property chain into border-width and outline-width while style states flip through non-painting frames.

edge_cases.rationale: The none/hidden probe explicitly separates the border used paint width from the retained Typed OM computed width instead of treating getComputedStyle().borderTopWidth as the author-width source.

references.rationale: The ChromeStatus feature link for 5133099851317248 remains present.

design_system.rationale: The repaired UI uses shared CSS variables, no raw hex colors, and no source inline event handlers or inline style attributes.

summary: Open critique items repaired for this concept: the animation now includes the promised custom-property width chain and the exact CSSOM width/style probe. DevTools MCP verification exercised the contract width/style controls, duration slider, box-count slider, style-flip toggle, and pause/resume at http://localhost:3000/v147/css-update-decoupling-of-width-and-style-properties/animation-pipeline-demo/?verify=after-final; the hidden branch showed border Typed OM at 10px, getComputedStyle().borderTopWidth at 0px, and outline/column-rule widths retained at 10px. Console and network were clean, and the feature conformance route passed 7 of 7 assertions in Chrome 148.
